steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "emphasize a difference" between the two isthmuses. The correct sentence must explicitly contrast them — placing a distinct fact about each side by side — not define one, describe one alone, or give a general definition.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The Isthmus of Ierapetra is an isthmus, or land bridge.': Mentions only one isthmus and merely restates the definition. No contrast is drawn. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The Isthmus of Panama is located in Central America, while the Isthmus of Ierapetra is located in the Mediterranean Sea.': Names both isthmuses and uses "while" to place their different locations in direct contrast. This is the difference the goal requires. ✓
  • Choice C — 'There is an isthmus, or land bridge, located in Central America.': Mentions only one location without naming or comparing the other isthmus. No contrast. ✗
  • Choice D — 'An isthmus, or land bridge, is a strip of land that connects two larger pieces of land across an expanse of water.': Provides only the general definition of an isthmus — does not mention either isthmus by name or draw any contrast. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
